type_definitions.name: FsAdrFileAdapter
type_definitions.description: Filesystem adapter implementing the AdrFilePort secondary port. Walks knowledge/adr/*.md on the real filesystem; reads and YAML-parses file contents using AdrFrontMatterCodec internally. Encapsulates both I/O and parsing so the usecase layer receives domain AdrFrontMatter objects directly (CN-05). Follows the existing Fs-prefix adapter naming convention (FsTrackStore, FsCatalogueLoader, FsCatalogueSpecSignalsStore). Holds a codec instance and the ADR directory path.

type_definitions.name: AdrFrontMatterCodec
type_definitions.description: Infrastructure struct responsible for YAML parsing of ADR front-matter strings into AdrFrontMatter domain value objects. Uses the existing Frontmatter utility to extract the YAML block, then uses serde_yaml to deserialize into AdrFrontMatterDto before constructing domain types. For each AdrDecisionDto, dispatches on status: String to construct the correct domain typestate struct and wraps it in the matching AdrDecisionEntry variant (ProposedDecision | AcceptedDecision | ImplementedDecision | SupersededDecision | DeprecatedDecision), carrying state-specific fields (implemented_in into ImplementedDecision, superseded_by into SupersededDecision) and embedding AdrDecisionCommon for the shared grounds fields. Status strings that do not match a known variant surface as AdrFrontMatterCodecError::InvalidDecisionField. The codec enforces explicit-key-presence detection via a serde_yaml::Value intermediate pass (Option A): before typed DTO deserialization, the raw YAML block is parsed to serde_yaml::Value and each decision mapping is inspected for the explicit presence of the 'implemented_in' and 'superseded_by' keys regardless of their value (i.e. 'key: null' counts as key present). If either key is present on a status that does not own it, the codec immediately returns InvalidDecisionField — this is necessary because serde Option<String> deserialization cannot distinguish 'key: null' from 'key absent', so a typed forbidden-when check on Some(_) would silently accept 'implemented_in: null' on status 'proposed'. The explicit-key-presence check closes this gap: (a) forbidden-when-key-present — 'implemented_in' key present on any status other than 'implemented' → InvalidDecisionField; 'superseded_by' key present on any status other than 'superseded' → InvalidDecisionField. After the key-presence pass succeeds, the typed DTO is deserialized and (b) required-when-value — status 'implemented' requires implemented_in: Some(_), status 'superseded' requires superseded_by: Some(_), else InvalidDecisionField. Together, (a) and (b) uphold the typestate invariant that state-specific payloads cannot appear (even as null) outside their owning state. serde(deny_unknown_fields) on AdrDecisionDto additionally rejects unknown schema fields. serde derives live here, not in domain (CN-05 hexagonal rule). Not a port adapter — it is a pure parsing utility struct with no port interface to implement. Kind use_case captures its role as a pure computation unit. Proxy expected_members omitted: the catalogue codec does not render expected_methods for use_case kind; the method semantics (fn parse(&self, yaml: &str) -> Result<AdrFrontMatter, AdrFrontMatterCodecError>) are described here in prose (documented codec gap).

type_definitions.name: AdrFrontMatterCodecError
type_definitions.description: Error type for YAML front-matter parse failures within AdrFrontMatterCodec. YamlParse wraps serde_yaml errors; MissingAdrId covers missing required adr_id field; InvalidDecisionField covers per-decision field validation failures. Infrastructure-layer concern — does not propagate serde types into domain.

type_definitions.name: AdrFrontMatterDto
type_definitions.description: Serde-derived DTO mirroring the YAML front-matter schema (adr_id + decisions[] array). Used as the intermediate deserialization target inside AdrFrontMatterCodec before converting to domain AdrFrontMatter value object. serde(deny_unknown_fields) to fail on schema drift. Infrastructure-only — never exported to domain or usecase layers.

type_definitions.name: AdrDecisionDto
type_definitions.description: Serde-derived DTO for a single element of the decisions[] array. Covers all schema fields from D3: id (required), user_decision_ref (Option), review_finding_ref (Option), candidate_selection (Option), status (required String — validated and dispatched to AdrDecisionEntry variant by AdrFrontMatterCodec), superseded_by (Option<String>, required when status is 'superseded'), implemented_in (Option<String>, required when status is 'implemented'), grandfathered (Option<bool>, defaults to false). Note: serde Option<String> cannot distinguish 'key: null' from 'key absent'; AdrFrontMatterCodec performs an explicit-key-presence check on the raw serde_yaml::Value before deserializing this DTO, so that 'implemented_in: null' on a non-'implemented' status is caught as InvalidDecisionField before reaching this struct. AdrFrontMatterCodec converts this to the appropriate AdrDecisionEntry enum variant: unknown status values surface as AdrFrontMatterCodecError::InvalidDecisionField; misplaced state-specific fields (implemented_in key present on non-'implemented' status, superseded_by key present on non-'superseded' status) also surface as InvalidDecisionField via the key-presence pre-check to uphold the typestate invariant. Infrastructure-only — never exported to domain or usecase layers.

type_definitions.name: AdrSignalsVerifyAdapter
type_definitions.description: Top-level infrastructure-layer composition struct wired at the CLI composition root. Assembles VerifyAdrSignalsInteractor with FsAdrFileAdapter injected and exposes it for use by the CLI 'verify adr-signals' subcommand. This is a wiring/composition struct — it does not implement any secondary port. Named *Adapter to indicate it is the CLI-facing wiring point. Kind use_case captures its role as a composition unit with no port trait. This type lives in the infrastructure layer (declared in infrastructure-types.json); kind use_case is a catalogue classification (pure computation/wiring unit with no port interface), not a layer designation.
